channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Sam Altman offers clues about where OpenAI is headed
item.description: On Sunday OpenAI cofounder and CEO Sam Altman published a blog post titled Reflections about his company’s progress—and the speed bumps along the way—during its first nine years.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Online shopping grew to $1.2 trillion over the holiday season, Salesforce found
item.description: Online sales hit record highs—$1.2 trillion globally and $282 billion in the U.S.—over the holiday season, but high return rates could hit strong profit margins, according to new data from Salesforce.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Uber and Lyft to offer driverless rideshare cars
item.description: Customers in Atlanta and Austin, Texas, will be able to hail one of these driverless rideshare cars via the Uber app.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, NewsNation

item.title: NASA’s micro-mission Lunar Trailblazer will scout the Moon this year
item.description: NASA’s upcoming Artemis II mission is slated to return astronauts to the Moon no sooner than April 2026. Astronauts were last on the Moon in 1972 during the Apollo 17 mission.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: HP adds trio of Intel-powered EliteBook laptops as AI PC craze reaches fever pitch
item.description: HP’s EliteBook trio combines Intel-powered AI, sleek displays, and cutting-edge features for professionals.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: LG confirms the LG C5 and B5 OLED TVs are coming, but there's good news and bad news
item.description: I got to see LG's mid-range C5 OLED TV and to speak to LG about it – and I'm glad I did, because up 'til that point, the model hadn't been made official.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney’s Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Chapman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Unlocking the full potential of enterprise AI
item.description: Despite the huge promise surrounding AI, many organizations are finding their implementations are not delivering as hoped.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Malicious apps posing as VPNs can turn your device into a tool for cyberattacks
item.description: Not all apps out there offer legit virtual private networks, according to Kurt the CyberGuy. He explains how to keep your information safe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok creators left in limbo while awaiting decision on potential platform ban
item.description: The fate of TikTok is keeping creators and small business owners in anxious limbo as they await a decision from the Supreme Court that could upheld their livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr. And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Net neutrality never stood a chance
item.description: A federal appeals court sealed the fate of the Biden administration’s efforts to regulate internet providers on Thursday, striking down the Federal Communications Commission’s 2024 net neutrality order once and for all.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Dating Sunday 2025: The busiest day on dating apps is almost here
item.description: Dating apps are gearing up for their busiest day of the year: Dating Sunday. This landmark day in the dating world always lands on the first Sunday of January.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Linux filesystems: Ext4, Btrfs, XFS, ZFS and more
item.description: Linux supports quite a few filesystem types. Your choice of which filesystem to use can play an important role in optimizing performance, reliability and data integrity. Among the more popular filesystems are Ext4, Btrfs, XFS and ZFS. Each is widely used and offers some unique features and benefits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chinese cyber firm linked to botnet attack sanctioned in US
item.description: The federal government hit Chinese cyber group Integrity Technology Group with new sanctions on Friday, citing alleged computer intrusion incidents against those in the U.S. At the direction of the China, hackers associated with the Integrity Technology Group allegedly targeted multiple U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Indiana bill could force kids under 16 off of social media without parental permission
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS--- If State Sen. Mike Bohacek’s (R-Michiana Shores) bill passes next session, social media platforms would need to block kids under 16 from accessing their content if they don’t receive their parents’ permission to use their sites. ”All we’re trying to do is protect kids,” Bohacek said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Adamson, NewsNation

item.title: 4 keys for writing cross-platform apps
item.description: These days, most every application has the potential to be a cross-platform application. That’s great for users but often difficult for developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4
